message:
perf(vdbe): reuse MakeRecord buffer allocation across rows (bd-pt17g)

The make_record_buf Vec<u8> was taken via std::mem::take() on each
MakeRecord opcode, moving the allocation into the blob and leaving the
buffer with capacity 0. This forced a fresh heap allocation per row.

Fix: copy bytes into Arc<[u8]> via Arc::from(&buf[..]), then clear()
the buffer (keeps capacity, sets len=0). Subsequent rows reuse the
same allocation. For 10K-row INSERT, eliminates ~10K Vec allocations.

C SQLite equivalent: vdbe.c OP_MakeRecord writes into Mem.zMalloc
which is reused across rows without reallocation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
